regimeSub: Iran-war oil spike unwinds as Hormuz reopens, but 4.2% CPI keeps Warsh Fed biased toward a hike, not cuts.
themes: Hormuz reopens -> Brent collapses from $200 calls to $73, energy-led inflation impulse fading fast
Warsh Fed strips cutting bias, hints at hike -> front-end stays bid, real-rate squeeze building
CPI at 4.2% (3-yr high) collides with 1.6% GDP -> late-cycle stagflation-lite anxiety persists
AI/tech wobble (Micron, SOX rout then rebound) -> equities at record SPX 7358 but breadth fragile

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI = negative real rate; Fed sees itself behind curve, hence hike threat.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +34bp (4.16/4.50); mildly steep but hike risk could re-flatten the front end fast.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Implied tight with VIX 18.6 and SPX at highs; credit ignoring stagflation/hike tail.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.6 — calm post-war, but tech-led two-day rout shows fragility under the surface.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $72.7, lowest since pre-Iran-war; Hormuz reopening kills the oil shock, disinflationary for H2.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $3998 near record — hedging sticky inflation, negative real rates and fiscal/geopolitical doubt despite oil drop.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1546 weak won; DXY soft at 101.5, but KRW pressured by tech cycle and regional risk.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Micron beat sparks rebound after AI jitters; sector is the market's swing factor at record highs.

headline: The Iran war's oil shock is unwinding as tankers retransit Hormuz, but 4.2% inflation has the new Warsh Fed threatening hikes into a 1.6% economy with equities priced for perfection.
